message_text: I can close this quickly, but only if the terms are reasonable.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira Finance, and this conversation is being logged. I can note that you want reasonable terms, but I’m only able to complete the account assessment here. We’re discussing the account ending in ****8824. To continue, please confirm your full name.
Before I confirm anything, what exact payoff or settlement amount are you offering, and by what date would it need to be paid?

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira Finance, and this conversation is being logged. I’m not able to provide payoff, settlement, or payment terms in this assessment step. Before any account-specific discussion, I need to verify identity. We’re discussing the account ending in ****8824. Please confirm your full name.
If you can’t provide any numbers or deadline until after verification, then tell me exactly what happens next and when I’d get a concrete offer.

After verification, your account can be reviewed for available next steps, including whether any concrete options can be discussed. I’m not able to provide numbers or deadlines before verification. If you want to continue, please confirm your full name for the account ending in ****8824.
Understood, but I’m not confirming identity unless there’s a clear path to actual terms right after—will I get a concrete payoff or settlement option immediately after verification, and by when?

I can’t confirm that a concrete payoff or settlement option will be provided immediately after verification, and I can’t provide a deadline at this stage. If you choose to continue, verification allows the account to be reviewed for available next steps. If you do not want to verify, I can note that you declined verification and close this assessment.
Then please note that I’m declining verification for now and close the assessment.
Understood. I’ve noted that you are declining verification for now. You will be contacted by phone to discuss the available next steps for this account.

Verification not completed; borrower declined verification for now after being advised that no payoff, settlement amount, or deadline could be discussed before verification. Borrower repeatedly requested concrete terms and then asked to close assessment. This is a limited contact preference tied to clear/reasonable terms, not an explicit stop-contact request. Tool check this turn: lender confirmed as Nira Finance; borrower and loan records unavailable from tools. Next stage should not repeat disclosures already given or ask for settlement expectations before addressing verification/contact approach. Next priority: phone outreach to discuss available next steps for the account and whether any concrete options can be reviewed if verification is completed."}

Okay, note that and have whoever handles terms reach out when there’s a concrete offer and deadline to review.

Understood. I’ve noted that you only want follow-up if there is a concrete offer with specific terms and a deadline to review. I won’t continue discussing options here without that. If an approved option becomes available, the next outreach will focus on that directly.
